recentHighlights: Favor the Bold (S6E05) — The fleet assembles at Starbase 375. Sisko presents his plan to retake DS9. Odo, 3 days out of the Link, watches Solids from the Promenade and finds them 'insignificant' until the Female Founder overreaches: 'We'll have to break them of that' (freedom). That line snaps him back. Quark sits with the weight of having to save his brother and the quadrant. Ziyal definitively breaks from Dukat. 1,254 Dominion ships vs the Federation fleet. 'Fortune favors the bold.' Written by Behr & Beimler, directed by Rick Kolbe.

Behind the Lines (S6E04) — The Female Founder returns to seduce Odo back into the Link. 'The drop becomes the ocean.' Sisko promoted to adjutant, sidelined from the Defiant. Rom captured when Odo — lost in the Link — fails to disable the alarms. 'It just... didn't seem to matter.' The coldest moment in the series, directed by LeVar Burton. The episode that catalyzed the Flask Framework: safety-through-recognizability requires visible edges.

Rocks and Shoals (S6E03) — Sisko's crew stranded on a desolate planet with a dying Dax. Vorta Keevan manipulates them into ambushing his own Jem'Hadar for rescue. Remata'Klan's tragedy: 'It's not my life to give up. And it never was.' Sisko buries the Jem'Hadar with their disruptors planted as grave markers. Kira's awakening: 'I'm a collaborator, Odo.' Written by Ronald D. Moore, directed by Mike Vejar.

Sons and Daughters (S6E02) — Worf and Alexander's reckoning. Five years of silence broken in a Klingon Bird-of-Prey training room. Alexander tells his father the truth: 'You never accepted me. You abandoned me.' Martok's adoption ceremony — acceptance without conditions. Directed by Avery Brooks.

A Time to Stand (S6E01) — Season 6 premiere. Three months into the Dominion War. Seventh Fleet decimated (14 of 112 survived Tyra). Sisko relieved of Defiant command, given a covert mission: destroy the ketracel-white facility. Garak wears the VR headset. Odo accepts a Founder seat. Mission succeeds — shockwave cripples the ship, no warp drive. Written by Behr & Beimler, directed by Allan Kroeker.
